title: WorkspaceWorkflow
description: The persisted form of a designer working copy (workflow-designer design §4.1): a mutable Arazzo document (plus its designer UI state) saved as many times as needed during development without ever minting a catalog version. Reach-scoped via management tags like a registered source; publish mints an immutable catalog version from it. Backends store this document verbatim.

id.description: The server-assigned working-copy identity (opaque; stable for the working copy's lifetime).

name.description: A human-friendly display name for the working copy (e.g. 'nightly-reconcile — retry tuning'). Mutable.

baseWorkflowId.description: Provenance: the catalog base workflow this working copy was opened from, if any.

basedOnVersion.description: Provenance: the catalog version number the document (and its scenarios) were copied from, if any.

document.description: The Arazzo document under edit, stored and returned verbatim. The control plane validates it on demand (validateWorkingCopy), never silently rewrites it.

designerState.description: Designer UI state (node positions and similar) — a sibling of the document, never written into it and never packaged (design §5.2). Stored and returned verbatim.

sources.description: The sources attached to this working copy, keyed by sourceDescriptions name (workflow-designer design §4.1): a registry reference (resolved at read time) or an inline document (stored verbatim so the working copy resolves like a package).

scenarios.description: The working copy's test scenarios (workflow-designer design §4.2), stored verbatim and published into the package as metadata/scenarios.json. Shape is validated at the API contract; the store carries the set like the designer state.

gitBinding.description: Binds the working copy to a repository branch (workflow-designer design §4.7): the document (path), optionally its spec files (specPaths: sourceDescriptions name → repository path) and scenario files (scenariosDir, one <name>.scenario.json per scenario), round-trip with pull/commit on the caller's brokered token. Mutable: a save that includes it replaces it; one that omits it carries the stored binding forward.

managementTags.description: The security tags (KVP labels) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this working copy (§14.2). Immutable: set at create (the deployment stamps the creator's internal tenant tag), carried forward unchanged on update.

createdBy.description: The actor that created the working copy.

createdAt.description: When the working copy was created.

lastUpdatedBy.description: The actor that last saved the working copy, if any.

lastUpdatedAt.description: When the working copy was last saved, if ever.

etag.description: The optimistic-concurrency token; a save presents the etag it read and conflicts (409) when stale.

AttachedSource.title: AttachedSource
AttachedSource.description: One attachment: the sourceDescriptions name it satisfies, plus a registry reference OR an inline document.

name.description: The sourceDescriptions name this attachment satisfies.

kind.description: registry = resolves a registered source by name at read time; inline = the document is stored here.

sourceName.description: registry only: the registered source's name.

document.description: inline only: the source document, stored verbatim.

SecurityTagInfo.title: SecurityTagInfo
SecurityTagInfo.description: A security label (KVP) scoping the working copy for row authorization (§14.2).
